project.name: emergent-world-model-sandbox
project.description: A 2D grid-world artificial-life simulation where evolving neural agents (GRU Actor-Critic) learn survival strategies and exhibit emergent behaviors such as farming and cooperation, via dual-mode evolution (online RL with Lamarckian inheritance, or pure neuroevolution).

nodes.name: analyze_observation_sensitivity.py
nodes.summary: Diagnostic script that probes how an agent's brain output responds to perturbations of individual observation features, ranking feature sensitivity.

nodes.name: _create_probe_world
nodes.summary: Builds a minimal controlled world and agent used as a stable baseline for observation-sensitivity probing.

nodes.name: _measure
nodes.summary: Measures the change in brain action distribution when a single observation feature is perturbed.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point that orchestrates probe-world creation, feature measurement, and ranked sensitivity reporting.

nodes.name: main.py
nodes.summary: Primary simulation entry point that loads configuration, builds the world and agent population, runs the main loop, and dispatches rendering and logging.
nodes.languageNotes: Single monolithic main() of ~780 lines handling the full simulation lifecycle.

nodes.name: load_config
nodes.summary: Loads and parses the YAML simulation configuration file into a runtime config dictionary.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Drives the entire simulation: world initialization, population seeding, the per-tick update loop, evolution, rendering, and logging.

nodes.name: test_exploration_bonus.py
nodes.summary: Scenario test verifying that the exploration-bonus reward mechanism affects agent behavior as intended.

nodes.name: populate_world_with_resources
nodes.summary: Test helper that seeds a world with food and resources for exploration scenarios.

nodes.name: test_exploration_bonus
nodes.summary: Asserts that enabling the exploration bonus changes agent movement coverage over a simulation run.

nodes.name: test_calamity.py
nodes.summary: Tests the world calamity (mass extinction / resource collapse) mechanism and its triggering conditions.

nodes.name: test_calamity
nodes.summary: Verifies that a calamity event triggers under the configured conditions and affects population and resources.

nodes.name: test_object_registry.py
nodes.summary: Comprehensive unit-test suite for the object registry covering specs, definitions, CRUD, creation, builtins, categories, config loading, and backward compatibility.

nodes.name: TestObjectDefinition
nodes.summary: Tests construction, serialization round-trips, and dict-based loading of ObjectDefinition.

nodes.name: TestRegistryCreate
nodes.summary: Tests the registry's object-creation factory for berries, seeds, plants, fertilizer, and override handling.

nodes.name: TestCustomObjectTypes
nodes.summary: Tests defining custom object lifecycles (mushroom, healing herb) and multi-component objects via the registry.

nodes.name: test_observation_sanity.py
nodes.summary: Sanity tests confirming that the egocentric observation encoding rotates with agent facing and responds correctly to food position.

nodes.name: test_parallel.py
nodes.summary: Tests parallel vs serial agent updates and parallel execution of world systems for consistency and correctness.

nodes.name: TestParallelUpdate
nodes.summary: Verifies parallel and serial agent update modes run correctly and produce equivalent tick counts.

nodes.name: TestParallelWorldSystems
nodes.summary: Tests parallel execution of soil dynamics and plant systems, plus thread-safe logging offload.

nodes.name: test_resource_spawn_stacking.py
nodes.summary: Tests resource-spawn no-stacking rules and calamity respawn-rate behavior.

nodes.name: test_resource_spawn_respects_no_stacking
nodes.summary: Asserts the resource spawn system does not stack multiple objects on a single tile when stacking is disabled.

nodes.name: test_calamity_respawn_rate
nodes.summary: Verifies the post-calamity resource respawn rate matches the configured recovery behavior.

nodes.name: test_stacking_config.py
nodes.summary: Tests strict vs legacy object-stacking configuration modes and the agent drop action under each.

nodes.name: test_strict_mode
nodes.summary: Verifies strict stacking mode prevents multiple objects from occupying the same tile.

nodes.name: test_agent_drop_action
nodes.summary: Tests that an agent's drop action respects stacking configuration constraints.

nodes.name: test_systems.py
nodes.summary: Unit-test suite for world ecosystem systems: plant growth, seed germination, decay, fertilizer, resource spawning, and the system manager.

nodes.name: TestPlantGrowthSystem
nodes.summary: Tests plant aging, death, and multi-plant handling within the plant growth system.

nodes.name: TestWorldSystemManager
nodes.summary: Verifies the world system manager runs all subsystems in the correct order each tick.

nodes.name: test_tile_effects.py
nodes.summary: Extensive test suite for tile effects (sand terrain), covering interaction/tile-effect specs, growth/germination multipliers, sand spread, and terrain reclamation.

nodes.name: TestTileEffectSystem
nodes.summary: Tests the tile-effect system's fertility/moisture clamping and spread mechanics including blockers and exposure tracking.

nodes.name: TestReclamation
nodes.summary: Tests terrain reclamation: sand reverting to soil after intervals, fertility restoration, and blocker-driven counter resets.

nodes.name: test_world.py
nodes.summary: Core unit tests for Tile, WorldObject, and World, covering tiles, object management, movement, neighbors, and reproducible terrain generation.

nodes.name: TestWorld
nodes.summary: Tests World creation, validation, object add/remove/move, neighbor queries, updates, and deterministic terrain generation.

nodes.name: render.py
nodes.summary: Console (ASCII) renderer for the world grid, mapping tiles, objects, and agents to characters for terminal display.

nodes.name: ConsoleRenderer
nodes.summary: Renders the world as an ASCII grid to the terminal and prints per-tile info.

nodes.name: gpu_renderer.py
nodes.summary: GPU-accelerated isometric renderer that draws the world, objects, and agents using instanced geometry, with HUD, tooltips, and camera controls.
nodes.languageNotes: Single large IsometricRenderer class managing geometry buffers, shader uniforms, and an interactive render loop.

nodes.name: IsometricRenderer
nodes.summary: Builds instanced tile/object/agent geometry, manages camera and coordinate transforms, and runs the GPU-accelerated isometric render loop with HUD overlays.

nodes.name: pygame_renderer.py
nodes.summary: Pygame-based 2D renderer with a pannable/zoomable camera, world drawing, HUD, tile info display, and interactive event loop.

nodes.name: Camera
nodes.summary: Pannable, zoomable camera handling world-to-screen and screen-to-world coordinate transforms.

nodes.name: PygameRenderer
nodes.summary: Renders the world, HUD, and tile info via Pygame and runs the interactive event/render loop.

nodes.name: object_registry.py
nodes.summary: Data-driven registry of object definitions and component specs (edible, seed, plant, fertilizer, tool, physics, interaction, tile-effect) with creation, lookup, and YAML config loading.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses dataclass-style spec objects plus a central ObjectRegistry acting as a factory and category resolver.

nodes.name: TileEffectSpec
nodes.summary: Spec describing a tile/terrain effect: growth, germination, and spawn multipliers, spread behavior, and fertility/moisture overrides.

nodes.name: ObjectDefinition
nodes.summary: Composite definition of an object type aggregating its component specs, with dict serialization round-trips.

nodes.name: ObjectRegistry
nodes.summary: Central registry that registers, looks up, and instantiates world objects from definitions, resolving categories, physics, observation encoding, and tile effects, and loading from config.

nodes.name: register_builtin_objects
nodes.summary: Registers the built-in object definitions (berry, seed, plant, fertilizer, etc.) into a registry instance.

nodes.name: objects.py
nodes.summary: Defines the component-entity model for world objects: base Component, typed components (edible, seed, plant, fertilizer, tool), and the WorldObject container.
nodes.languageNotes: Lightweight entity-component system where WorldObject aggregates typed Component subclasses.

nodes.name: PlantComponent
nodes.summary: Component tracking a plant's growth stage and maturity/alive state.

nodes.name: WorldObject
nodes.summary: Entity container that holds typed components and supports add/get/has/remove component operations.

nodes.name: systems.py
nodes.summary: Ecosystem update systems (plant growth, seed germination, decay, fertilizer, soil dynamics, resource spawn, tile effects) coordinated by a WorldSystemManager.
nodes.languageNotes: Each system follows a uniform update(world) interface; module-level helpers compute tile-effect multipliers.

nodes.name: SeedGerminationSystem
nodes.summary: System that germinates seeds resting on suitable soil after sufficient time, modulated by tile effects.

nodes.name: DecaySystem
nodes.summary: System that decreases object freshness, removes spoiled objects, and drops seeds from spoiled berries.

nodes.name: SoilDynamicsSystem
nodes.summary: System updating soil fertility and moisture dynamics across tile rows, supporting parallel row updates.

nodes.name: ResourceSpawnSystem
nodes.summary: System that spawns berries from mature plants and performs safety spawns when resources are depleted.

nodes.name: WorldSystemManager
nodes.summary: Orchestrates all ecosystem systems, running them in a defined order on each world tick.

nodes.name: TileEffectSystem
nodes.summary: System managing terrain tile effects such as sand spread, fertility/moisture overrides, and reclamation back to soil.

nodes.name: tiles.py
nodes.summary: Defines the TerrainType enum and the Tile class with fertility, passability, plantability, and object management.

nodes.name: Tile
nodes.summary: Grid cell holding terrain type, fertility, contained objects, and passability/plantability logic.

nodes.name: world.py
nodes.summary: Central World class managing the tile grid, object and agent lifecycle, terrain generation, per-tick updates, calamity, caching, and learning-budget control.
nodes.languageNotes: Large hub class coordinating tiles, systems, agents, and serial/parallel update paths.

nodes.name: World
nodes.summary: The simulation world: holds the tile grid, manages objects/agents, generates terrain, runs system and agent updates (serial/parallel), and handles calamity and caching.

nodes.name: actions.py
nodes.summary: Defines the discrete Action enum and the immutable ActionResult record used to describe an agent's chosen action and its outcome (success, energy cost, target, interaction kind).

nodes.name: Action
nodes.summary: Enumeration of the discrete actions an agent can take (move, turn, pick up, drop, eat, use, wait).

nodes.name: ActionResult
nodes.summary: Immutable NamedTuple capturing the result of executing an action, including success flag, energy cost, affected object and interaction metadata.

nodes.name: agent.py
nodes.summary: Core Agent class orchestrating per-tick perception, brain-driven decision making, action execution, energy/death handling, reproduction, and optional online learning with Lamarckian knowledge inheritance.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy delegation to agent_utils for action execution and to Brain for decisions; uses class-level logger and world_model_logger attributes shared across instances.

nodes.name: Agent
nodes.summary: Represents a living simulation agent: holds genome, brain, energy and inventory, and implements the observe-decide-act loop, death/respawn of carried items, sexual reproduction, and learning lifecycle hooks.

nodes.name: brain.py
nodes.summary: Implements the GRU-based Actor-Critic neural network that maps observations and hidden state to action probabilities and a value estimate, with temperature-controlled stochastic action selection.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure-NumPy GRU forward pass; weights are unpacked from a flat genome vector via brain_utils.

nodes.name: Brain
nodes.summary: GRU Actor-Critic policy network that runs forward passes, samples masked actions with temperature, and exposes weight-count and action-preference utilities.

nodes.name: genome.py
nodes.summary: Defines the Genome holding a flat weight vector plus heritable traits, with random initialization, multiple crossover strategies, Gaussian weight mutation, and trait inheritance for neuroevolution.

nodes.name: Genome
nodes.summary: Encapsulates an agent's heritable weights and traits, supporting uniform/one-point/blend crossover, mutation, copying, and lineage tracking for evolutionary reproduction.

nodes.name: create_default_trait_config
nodes.summary: Returns the default trait configuration mapping trait names to their value ranges used when randomly initializing genomes.

nodes.name: test_anti_spin.py
nodes.summary: Test verifying the reward shaper penalizes repetitive spinning behavior so agents that turn in place accumulate lower rewards than those moving productively.

nodes.name: test_anti_spin
nodes.summary: Builds an agent and reward shaper, simulates spinning vs. non-spinning action sequences, and asserts the spinning sequence earns lower shaped reward.

nodes.name: test_brain_v2.py
nodes.summary: Unit test suite for the GRU Brain covering weight-count calculation, initialization, forward pass shapes, action decision, masking, temperature sampling, hidden-state continuity, and weight packing round-trips.

nodes.name: TestBrainV2
nodes.summary: Pytest class exercising the Brain network's correctness across forward passes, masking, temperature-based sampling, and deterministic weight reconstruction.

nodes.name: test_energy_sustainability.py
nodes.summary: Integration test populating a world with food and agents, running the simulation, and checking that the agent population can sustain energy levels over time.

nodes.name: test_energy_sustainability
nodes.summary: Loads config, spawns berries and agents into a World, advances many ticks, and asserts agents survive and maintain viable energy distributions.

nodes.name: test_genome.py
nodes.summary: Unit test suite for the Genome covering random creation, construction from weights, copying, the three crossover strategies, mutation, lineage tracking, weight statistics, and trait inheritance.

nodes.name: TestGenome
nodes.summary: Pytest class validating genome initialization, crossover variants, mutation effects, lineage metadata, and trait blending behavior.

nodes.name: test_logging.py
nodes.summary: Test verifying the AgentLogger writes action and state log files with expected headers and rows when agents are updated in a world.

nodes.name: test_agent_logging
nodes.summary: Spawns agents in a world with an AgentLogger, advances ticks, and asserts action and state CSV files are created and populated.

nodes.name: test_turn_balance.py
nodes.summary: Tests that initial agent directions are diverse and roughly uniform, that turning instinct follows food location, and that the reward shaper penalizes dominant one-sided turning.

nodes.name: test_turn_balance_penalty_for_dominant_left
nodes.summary: Runs reward shapers over left-dominant, balanced, and right-dominant turn sequences and asserts the lopsided turning is penalized relative to balanced turning.

nodes.name: _build_obs_food_left
nodes.summary: Constructs a synthetic observation vector encoding food positioned to the agent's left for instinct testing.

nodes.name: _build_obs_food_right
nodes.summary: Constructs a synthetic observation vector encoding food positioned to the agent's right for instinct testing.

nodes.name: test_instinct_favors_left_when_food_is_left
nodes.summary: Averages brain action preferences over random genomes given food-left observations and asserts a left-turn instinct bias emerges.

nodes.name: test_instinct_favors_right_when_food_is_right
nodes.summary: Averages brain action preferences over random genomes given food-right observations and asserts a right-turn instinct bias emerges.

nodes.name: test_world_model_logger.py
nodes.summary: Test verifying the WorldModelLogger records state transitions and world-state snapshots to CSV files with valid headers and content.

nodes.name: test_world_model_logger
nodes.summary: Builds a world with an agent, logs observation-action-observation transitions and world states, then asserts the transitions and world-state CSV files exist and contain rows.

nodes.name: agent_utils.py
nodes.summary: Stateless helper functions implementing the agent action space: computes the action mask and executes movement, turning, pick up, drop, eat, use, and wait against the world.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure functions taking (agent, world) so action logic stays decoupled from the Agent class.

nodes.name: get_action_mask
nodes.summary: Computes a boolean mask of currently legal actions for an agent by inspecting passability, pickable objects, plantable tiles, and inventory contents.

nodes.name: execute_move_forward
nodes.summary: Moves the agent one tile in its facing direction if the target tile is passable, returning an ActionResult with energy cost.

nodes.name: execute_pick_up
nodes.summary: Picks up a pickable object from the agent's current or facing tile, adding it to inventory and returning the interaction outcome.

nodes.name: execute_drop
nodes.summary: Drops an inventory item onto the facing tile or a nearby free tile, handling stacking rules and returning an ActionResult.

nodes.name: execute_eat
nodes.summary: Consumes an edible item from inventory, restoring agent energy and removing the consumed object.

nodes.name: execute_use
nodes.summary: Applies a context-dependent use interaction such as planting seeds on plantable tiles or applying fertilizer, returning the resulting world change.

nodes.name: evolution.py
nodes.summary: Implements the neuroevolution loop: parent selection, agent cloning with weight mutation, and generation of the next population using adaptive mutation rates.

nodes.name: EvolutionConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration dataclass holding evolution hyperparameters such as population size, mutation rate, elitism, and selection pressure.

nodes.name: select_parents
nodes.summary: Selects the fittest agents from the population to serve as parents for the next generation via fitness-sorted truncation.

nodes.name: clone_agent
nodes.summary: Deep-copies a parent agent into a child, optionally mutating its genome weights and rebuilding the brain and initial hidden state.

nodes.name: mutate_genome_weights
nodes.summary: Applies Gaussian noise to a genome's flattened weight vector to introduce heritable variation.

nodes.name: next_generation
nodes.summary: Produces the next generation by carrying over elites and breeding mutated offspring from selected parents using adaptive mutation std.

nodes.name: learning.py
nodes.summary: Online reinforcement-learning engine for agents, providing an Actor-Critic learner with both NumPy and Torch vectorized backends and CPU/GPU/MPS device resolution.
nodes.languageNotes: Maintains parallel NumPy and PyTorch implementations of the forward/backward pass, switching backends at runtime based on device availability.

nodes.name: AgentLearner
nodes.summary: Per-agent Actor-Critic learner that stores experiences in a replay buffer, runs vectorized policy/value updates, and syncs learned weights back into the genome.

nodes.name: observation.py
nodes.summary: Thin compatibility shim that re-exports the perception/observation building utilities from utils.agents.perception.

nodes.name: test_evolution.py
nodes.summary: Integration test/harness that runs multiple generations of evolving agents in a populated world and reports fitness and diversity statistics.

nodes.name: populate_world_with_resources
nodes.summary: Seeds a test world with berries, plants, and seeds at random valid positions to provide food resources for evolving agents.

nodes.name: create_initial_population
nodes.summary: Builds an initial population of agents with random genomes and brains sized to the observation/action space, enabling online learning.

nodes.name: run_generation
nodes.summary: Runs a single generation by spawning agents into a fresh world and ticking the simulation for a fixed number of steps.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Driver that runs the full multi-generation evolution loop, computing fitness, recording statistics, and printing a summary.

nodes.name: test_loop_reward_shaping.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests verifying RewardShaper behavior for proactive turning after straight runs and stronger penalties for backtracking than forward progress.

nodes.name: test_proactive_turn_reward_after_straight_run
nodes.summary: Asserts that the reward shaper grants a proactive-turn bonus after a sustained straight run of movement.

nodes.name: test_backtrack_is_penalized_more_than_forward_progress
nodes.summary: Asserts that backtracking to a recently visited position incurs a larger penalty than the reward for forward progress.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel that aggregates and re-exports agent helper utilities (perception, brain, evolution, learning, and action helpers) for convenient import.

nodes.name: brain_utils.py
nodes.summary: Pure NumPy neural-network primitives for the agent brain: activation functions, weight-count calculation, flat-weight unpacking, and a single GRU step.

nodes.name: calculate_weight_count
nodes.summary: Computes the total number of trainable weights for a brain given encoder layers, GRU hidden size, and output size.

nodes.name: unpack_weights
nodes.summary: Reshapes a flat genome weight vector into structured encoder, GRU gate, policy-head, and value-head parameter matrices.

nodes.name: gru_step
nodes.summary: Performs one GRU recurrent step computing reset, update, and candidate gates to produce the next hidden state.

nodes.name: evolution_utils.py
nodes.summary: Evolution support utilities: fitness calculation, adaptive mutation-rate scheduling, and an EvolutionStats tracker for population diversity and history.

nodes.name: calculate_fitness
nodes.summary: Computes an agent's fitness score from survival, energy, and behavioral factors.

nodes.name: adaptive_mutation_std
nodes.summary: Adapts the mutation standard deviation based on population diversity and fitness stagnation, clamped to a configured range.

nodes.name: EvolutionStats
nodes.summary: Tracks per-generation evolution metrics (fitness, age, survival rate, genome diversity) and prints a formatted run summary.

nodes.name: learning_utils.py
nodes.summary: Core RL support classes: Experience tuples, a ReplayBuffer, a large multi-signal RewardShaper, and a BestAgentTracker that persists top genomes to disk.

nodes.name: ReplayBuffer
nodes.summary: Fixed-capacity experience replay buffer supporting add, random sampling, and clearing for the Actor-Critic learner.

nodes.name: RewardShaper
nodes.summary: Computes shaped rewards from environment interactions, combining exploration bonuses, food-proximity signals, anti-spin/anti-backtrack penalties, and action history.

nodes.name: BestAgentTracker
nodes.summary: Tracks the best-performing agents by fitness and saves/loads their genome weights to disk for Lamarckian inheritance and seeding.

nodes.name: perception.py
nodes.summary: Builds an agent's observation vector by encoding agent state, an egocentric vision grid, environmental stimulus signals, and inventory contents.

nodes.name: build_observation
nodes.summary: Assembles the full observation array for an agent by concatenating encoded agent-state, vision, stimulus, and inventory features.

nodes.name: _encode_agent_state
nodes.summary: Encodes the agent's internal state (energy, direction one-hot, inventory fill) into normalized observation features.

nodes.name: _encode_vision
nodes.summary: Builds an egocentric vision grid around the agent, encoding each visible tile rotated to the agent's facing direction.

nodes.name: _encode_tile
nodes.summary: Encodes a single tile's terrain and any object on it (edible/seed/plant values) into a feature vector for the vision grid.

nodes.name: _compute_observation_value
nodes.summary: Derives a normalized scalar observation value for a world object from its edible, seed, or plant component.

nodes.name: _encode_stimulus
nodes.summary: Computes directional stimulus signals (food gradients, distances, and presence flags) relative to the agent's position and facing.

nodes.name: _encode_inventory
nodes.summary: Encodes the agent's held inventory item, including its type and component-derived values, into observation features.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the agents module, re-exporting Agent, Brain, Genome, Action/ActionResult, AgentLearner, and shared agent utilities as the public agent API.
nodes.languageNotes: Python package __init__ acting as a public-API aggregator via re-exports.

nodes.name: test_baseline_easy_config.py
nodes.summary: Scenario test that loads the easy training config, populates a world with berries, plants, and seeds, runs learning-enabled agents, and verifies baseline survival behavior with logging.

nodes.name: test_baseline_verification.py
nodes.summary: Scenario test that builds a resource-populated world and verifies baseline agent behavior over multiple ticks, capturing world-model logs for inspection.

nodes.name: test_action_energy_shaping.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests validating action energy-cost shaping rules: escalating costs for turn and wait streaks, post-turn move discounts, fitness preservation on successful turns, and absence of forward-move bias in the brain.

nodes.name: test_drop_action_mask.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the drop-action mask in strict stacking mode, checking the mask disables dropping when no space is available and enables it when a neighboring tile is empty.

nodes.name: test_exploration_sweep.py
nodes.summary: Experiment harness that sweeps the exploration-bonus parameter by rewriting config, running simulations, and comparing eating success and action distributions across bonus values.

nodes.name: test_reproduction.py
nodes.summary: Integration test that loads config, seeds a world with abundant resources, and runs energy-rich agents for many ticks to verify reproduction events and population dynamics.

nodes.name: test_reproduction_config.py
nodes.summary: Tests that reproduction parameters are correctly loaded from config and applied to agents, plus fallback behavior verifying sensible defaults when no reproduction config is present.

nodes.name: populate_world_with_resources
nodes.summary: Randomly scatters berries, plants, and seeds across the world using config-driven counts, respecting valid and plantable tile constraints.

nodes.name: test_baseline_easy_config
nodes.summary: Drives the end-to-end baseline scenario: builds a world, spawns learning agents from random genomes, steps the simulation, and logs all states.

nodes.name: populate_world_with_resources
nodes.summary: Populates a world with parameterized counts of berries, plants, and seeds on valid and plantable tiles.

nodes.name: test_baseline_verification
nodes.summary: Runs a multi-tick baseline simulation with verbose progress output and world-model logging to verify agents behave as expected.

nodes.name: _create_agent_on_passable_tile
nodes.summary: Test helper that constructs an agent from a random genome and places it on a passable tile with passable neighbors for movement tests.

nodes.name: test_brain_no_move_forward_bias
nodes.summary: Verifies that a zero-weight brain produces no inherent bias toward the move-forward action when fed neutral observations.

nodes.name: _create_agent_on_passable_tile
nodes.summary: Test helper that spawns an agent on the first passable tile for drop-mask scenarios.

nodes.name: populate_world_with_resources
nodes.summary: Scatters parameterized counts of berries, plants, and seeds across the world for exploration-bonus experiments.

nodes.name: set_exploration_bonus
nodes.summary: Rewrites the exploration-bonus value directly in the agent source file via regex substitution to parameterize the sweep.

nodes.name: run_test
nodes.summary: Runs a single simulation for a given exploration-bonus value, collects action logs into a DataFrame, and computes eating-success metrics.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full exploration-bonus sweep across multiple values, prints comparative results, and restores the original bonus afterward.

nodes.name: load_config
nodes.summary: Loads and returns the YAML simulation config from the given path, raising if the file is missing.

nodes.name: test_reproduction
nodes.summary: Runs a long simulation with abundant resources and energetic agents to confirm reproduction occurs, tracking reproduction events, fitness, and population over many ticks.

nodes.name: test_reproduction_config_loading
nodes.summary: Loads reproduction settings from config, applies them to a world and agent, and asserts the configured reproduction parameters take effect.

nodes.name: test_without_config
nodes.summary: Verifies that default reproduction behavior applies sensibly when a world is created without an explicit reproduction config.

nodes.name: test_async_logger.py
nodes.summary: Pytest suite validating the AsyncWorldModelLogger, covering basic CSV output, write performance, buffer batching, world-state snapshots, and episode-end logging.

nodes.name: test_async_logger_basic
nodes.summary: Verifies that logging transitions through AsyncWorldModelLogger produces a non-empty transitions CSV file with the expected rows.

nodes.name: test_async_logger_performance
nodes.summary: Benchmarks the asynchronous logger by timing many log_transition calls and asserting throughput, confirming non-blocking write behavior.

nodes.name: test_async_logger_batching
nodes.summary: Confirms that buffered log entries are batched and flushed to disk by the background writer thread within the expected timing.

nodes.name: test_async_logger_world_state
nodes.summary: Checks that log_world_state captures aggregate world snapshots (agents, objects, soil) into the world-state CSV.

nodes.name: test_async_logger_episode_end
nodes.summary: Validates that terminal transitions trigger an episode-end record summarizing agent lifetime statistics in the episodes CSV.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for utils.data that re-exports the AsyncWorldModelLogger for convenient imports.

nodes.name: async_logger.py
nodes.summary: Implements AsyncWorldModelLogger, a background-thread CSV logger that records agent transitions, episode summaries, and world-state snapshots for world-model training data.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a queue.Queue plus a dedicated writer thread and deque buffers to decouple logging from the simulation loop for low-latency, batched CSV writes.

nodes.name: AsyncWorldModelLogger
nodes.summary: Thread-backed logger that queues transition, episode-end, and world-state events, batching them onto deque buffers and flushing periodically to CSV files for offline world-model training.
nodes.languageNotes: Producer/consumer pattern: log_* methods enqueue rows non-blockingly while a daemon writer thread drains the queue and flushes buffered rows to disk.

nodes.name: ci.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions CI workflow that runs on push and pull requests to main, executing lint (black + flake8), the pytest test suite across Python 3.11 and 3.12 with coverage, and a non-blocking mypy type check.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a build matrix to run tests across multiple Python versions; test and type-check steps are non-blocking via '|| true', and SDL2 system packages are installed for the pygame-based renderer.

nodes.name: lint
nodes.summary: Lint job that checks code formatting with black --check and runs flake8 across the repository on Python 3.12.

nodes.name: test
nodes.summary: Test job that installs SDL2 system libraries and Python dependencies, then runs the pytest suite with coverage across a 3.11/3.12 matrix, uploading the coverage report artifact on 3.12.

nodes.name: type-check
nodes.summary: Type-check job that runs mypy in non-blocking mode against the agents, world, and utils packages.

nodes.name: release.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions release workflow triggered on version tag pushes (v*) that generates a changelog from git history and publishes a GitHub Release.
nodes.languageNotes: Triggered exclusively by tag pushes matching 'v*'; uses fetch-depth 0 to access full git history for changelog generation and requires contents:write permission to publish releases.

nodes.name: release
nodes.summary: Release job that derives a changelog from commits since the previous tag and creates a GitHub Release via softprops/action-gh-release.

nodes.name: CHANGELOG.md
nodes.summary: Project changelog tracking unreleased additions and changes following the Keep a Changelog convention.

nodes.name: CLI_GUIDE.md
nodes.summary: Command-line usage guide covering installation, running simulations with different visualization modes, configuration, testing, and troubleshooting.

nodes.name: ECOSYSTEM.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive technical reference (124 sections) documenting the world architecture, ECS components, agent system, ecosystem physics, world systems, calamity/population control, configuration reference, and the reinforcement learning system.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Primary project overview documenting features, quick-start installation, architecture, neural agent design, evolution system, custom objects, data analysis, and visualization.

nodes.name: SUGGESTIONS.md
nodes.summary: Roadmap and suggestions document outlining tuning ideas, architecture/learning improvements, world expansion, robotics-platform and online-world tracks, and a phased progression plan.

nodes.name: WORLD_MODEL_IMPLEMENTATION_GUIDE.md
nodes.summary: Detailed implementation guide for adding learned world models (forward/inverse dynamics, intrinsic curiosity, model-based planning) including code samples and integration points in learning.py and main.py.

nodes.name: WORLD_MODEL_LOGGING_FORMAT.md
nodes.summary: Specification of the CSV logging format (transitions, episodes, world states) emitted for world-model training, including the 64-dimension observation vector layout and loading examples.

nodes.name: guideline.md
nodes.summary: Development guidelines covering Python environment management, code quality, architecture, testing, git workflow, error handling, and project-specific conventions.

nodes.name: requirements.txt
nodes.summary: Python dependency manifest pinning the packages (NumPy, pygame, PyYAML, and related libraries) required to run the simulation, renderers, and analysis tools.

nodes.name: todo.md
nodes.summary: Phase-by-phase TODO and progress log tracking completed milestones across core implementation, visualization, reinforcement learning, object stacking, reproduction config, population control, and resource spawn fixes.

nodes.name: custom_objects.yaml
nodes.summary: Declarative definitions of custom world object types (edibles, seeds, plants, terrain) loaded into the object registry to populate the simulation with user-defined entities at startup.
nodes.languageNotes: Each key under `objects:` becomes a unique type_id with optional spec sections (edible, seed, plant, spawn) that map onto ObjectDefinition fields in the registry.

nodes.name: default.yaml
nodes.summary: Primary simulation configuration covering world size, terrain, agents, brain, evolution, plants, resources, soil, learning, reproduction, calamity, and logging parameters that drive the default run.
nodes.languageNotes: Organized into 16 top-level sections, each mapping to a subsystem consumed by World and the agent stack.

nodes.name: training_easy.yaml
nodes.summary: Easier-difficulty simulation preset tuning resources, action costs, evolution, and learning to accelerate early agent training and survival.
nodes.languageNotes: Mirrors default.yaml's section layout with relaxed parameters; adds an action_costs section for energy-cost shaping.

nodes.name: .flake8
nodes.summary: Flake8 linter configuration defining line-length limits, ignored rules, and excluded paths enforced by the project's CI lint stage.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Ignore-list controlling which files the Understand-Anything knowledge-graph tooling skips during analysis.

nodes.name: config.json
nodes.summary: Minimal Understand-Anything tooling config that pins the output language for generated knowledge-graph content.

nodes.name: analyze_v3_1.py
nodes.summary: Standalone post-run analytics script that loads a simulation log into a pandas DataFrame and computes loop, energy, population, lifespan, spatial, temporal, farming, behavioral-diversity, efficiency, and action-sequence metrics.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy pandas/numpy use with per-agent groupby aggregations and Counter-based n-gram tallies; all metric functions are private helpers operating on a shared DataFrame.

nodes.name: _ensure_interaction_fields
nodes.summary: Normalizes and back-fills interaction-related DataFrame columns (object ids, types, interaction kinds, target coordinates) by parsing free-text log messages with regex extraction.

nodes.name: _compute_loop_metrics
nodes.summary: Detects repetitive movement loops per agent from position sequences and aggregates loop-frequency and severity metrics across the population.

nodes.name: _compute_energy_metrics
nodes.summary: Computes per-action energy costs and aggregate energy gain/loss balance across the simulation log.

nodes.name: _compute_population_dynamics
nodes.summary: Derives population dynamics (per-tick agent counts, births, deaths, turnover) from agent first/last-seen ticks.

nodes.name: _compute_lifespan_metrics
nodes.summary: Aggregates per-agent lifespan, peak fitness, and eat/pickup counts into mean/median/max summary statistics and a top-performer ranking.

nodes.name: _compute_spatial_metrics
nodes.summary: Measures spatial exploration via unique tiles visited per agent and net displacement, plus most-frequented tiles across the population.

nodes.name: _compute_temporal_phases
nodes.summary: Splits the run into temporal phases and reports per-phase action distributions, active-agent counts, and mean energy.

nodes.name: _compute_farming_metrics
nodes.summary: Quantifies farming behavior by counting seed-planting and seed-pickup interactions and the fraction of agents engaging in them.

nodes.name: _compute_behavioral_diversity
nodes.summary: Computes Shannon-entropy of each agent's action distribution to measure behavioral diversity and identifies most/least diverse agents.

nodes.name: _compute_efficiency_metrics
nodes.summary: Derives efficiency ratios such as energy spent per move and successful-move rate from the action log.

nodes.name: _compute_action_sequences
nodes.summary: Tallies the most common action bigrams and trigrams per agent to surface recurring behavioral motifs.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package marker for the simulation module documenting its scope (runner, configuration, generation tracking) and pinning the version.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package marker that makes the tests directory an importable Python package.

nodes.name: conftest.py
nodes.summary: Pytest configuration that injects the project root onto sys.path and provides a shared clean_world fixture returning a fresh 10x10 World.

nodes.name: clean_world
nodes.summary: Pytest fixture that constructs and returns a fresh 10x10 World instance for test isolation.

nodes.name: test_improved_rewards.py
nodes.summary: Integration-style test that shells out via subprocess to run the simulation with improved reward shaping and reports the outcome.

nodes.name: test_improved_rewards
nodes.summary: Runs the simulation as a subprocess to validate the improved reward configuration and prints diagnostic summaries.

nodes.name: test_max_population.py
nodes.summary: Test that loads the YAML config and verifies the reproduction max-population settings are present and sensible.

nodes.name: test_max_population
nodes.summary: Loads the simulation YAML config and checks the reproduction section's max-population parameters.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package marker for the utils module describing its scope (rendering, visualization, data logging, helpers) and pinning the version.

nodes.name: parallel.py
nodes.summary: Parallel agent-update pipeline that splits each tick into observe/decide, serial execute, and learn phases using a shared ThreadPoolExecutor to exploit NumPy GIL release.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor with lazily-created shared pools and TYPE_CHECKING-guarded imports to avoid circular dependencies with Agent and World.

nodes.name: _observe_and_decide
nodes.summary: Phase-1 worker that builds an agent's observation, computes its action mask, runs the brain forward pass, and returns the chosen action without mutating the world.

nodes.name: _execute_and_log
nodes.summary: Phase-2 worker that executes an agent's chosen action, re-observes the world, computes the shaped reward, and logs the transition for world-model training.

nodes.name: _learn_step
nodes.summary: Phase-3 worker that stores the agent's experience and runs a gradient update on its brain when a learning slot is available.

nodes.name: _handle_death
nodes.summary: Handles an agent's death by logging the terminal transition, storing the final experience, and removing or resetting the agent.

nodes.name: update_agents_parallel
nodes.summary: Orchestrates a full parallel agent-update tick across the three phases, handling deaths, reproduction, and submitting learn tasks to the thread pool.

nodes.name: get_pool
nodes.summary: Lazily creates and returns the shared compute ThreadPoolExecutor used for parallel agent phases.

nodes.name: get_io_pool
nodes.summary: Lazily creates and returns a separate ThreadPoolExecutor reserved for I/O-bound logging work.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package marker for the UI sub-module housing visualization components.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package marker for the world module describing its scope (world representation, tiles, objects, physics systems, object registry) and pinning the version.

layers.name: Entry Points & Analysis
layers.description: CLI entry point (main.py) and offline data-analysis scripts that parse simulation logs and report action distributions and survival metrics.

layers.name: World Engine
layers.description: The physically-consistent 2D grid world: tiles, world objects, the per-tick simulation systems (growth, decay, spawning, tile effects), the YAML-driven object registry, and the top-level World container.

layers.name: Agent Subsystem
layers.description: Agent lifecycle and decision-making: the Agent orchestrator, the GRU Actor-Critic Brain, the evolvable Genome, online RL learning, evolution, observation assembly, and primitive action definitions.

layers.name: Agent Support Utilities
layers.description: Stateless helper modules backing the agent subsystem: perception/observation encoding, action execution, brain, learning, and evolution utility functions.

layers.name: Rendering & Visualization
layers.description: Pygame 2D renderer and the GPU-accelerated ModernGL isometric 2.5D renderer, plus shared render helpers.

layers.name: Data & Logging
layers.description: Asynchronous, persistent-handle loggers that capture agent actions, states, and world-model transitions to CSV/other formats for analysis.

layers.name: Shared Utilities & Orchestration
layers.description: Cross-cutting utilities including the parallel agent-stepping engine and package-level orchestration/barrel modules.

layers.name: Test Suite
layers.description: Pytest unit and scenario tests covering world physics, agents, evolution, rewards, logging, configuration, and baseline behavior verification.

layers.name: Configuration
layers.description: YAML simulation/custom-object configs, the dependency manifest, and tooling configuration (flake8, understand-anything settings).

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Project README, CLI guide, changelog, roadmap/suggestions, world-model implementation and logging-format guides, and contributor guidelines.

layers.name: CI/CD Pipelines
layers.description: GitHub Actions workflows for linting, type-checking, testing, and release automation.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to understand the goal: a 2D grid world where neural agents evolve survival strategies and emergent behaviors (farming, cooperation) with no hardcoded high-level logic. The CLI guide and ecosystem doc expand on running modes and the object ecosystem.

tour.title: Application Entry Point
tour.description: main.py wires everything together: it parses CLI flags (--gui/--gpu, --mode, --config, --objects, --log), loads YAML config, builds the world and agent population, and runs the tick loop with the chosen renderer.

tour.title: The World Engine
tour.description: The World container holds the tile grid and all objects, advancing the simulation one tick at a time. This is the physically-consistent substrate everything else acts on.

tour.title: Objects, Systems & the Registry
tour.description: WorldObjects (food, seeds, plants, fertilizer, terrain) are defined data-first via the YAML-driven ObjectRegistry, and the per-tick systems evolve them — growth, decay, spawning, and tile effects like spreading sand. This is how new objects are added without code changes.

tour.title: The Agent
tour.description: Each Agent runs an observe-decide-act loop: it builds a 72-dim egocentric observation, asks its Brain for an action, and executes one of the masked primitive actions (MOVE/TURN/PICK_UP/DROP/EAT/USE/WAIT).

tour.title: The Brain & Genome
tour.description: The Brain is a compact GRU Actor-Critic network (~8.9k params) producing a policy over 8 actions plus a value estimate, with contextual instinct biases that fade as learning strengthens. The Genome encodes the evolvable weights and traits passed to offspring.

tour.title: Learning & Evolution (dual mode)
tour.description: In RL mode the AgentLearner trains the Actor-Critic online and syncs learned weights back to the genome (Lamarckian inheritance); evolution then performs tournament selection, crossover, and mutation. In neuroevolution mode gradients are off and only the genome evolves.

tour.title: Agent Support Utilities
tour.description: The agent subsystem leans on stateless helpers: perception encoders that build the observation vector, action-execution helpers, and brain/learning/evolution utility functions kept separate from the agent classes.

tour.title: Rendering & Visualization
tour.description: Two renderers visualize the world: a Pygame 2D orthographic view with HUD and tile inspector, and a GPU-accelerated ModernGL isometric 2.5D renderer with GLSL shaders and frustum culling.

tour.title: Data Logging & Analysis
tour.description: For headless research runs, the async world-model logger persists agent actions, states, and transition data; the analysis scripts then parse those logs to report action distributions and survival metrics against targets.

tour.title: Performance: Parallel Stepping
tour.description: utils/parallel.py implements the multi-phase parallel agent-stepping engine used to keep tick throughput high as the population grows.

tour.title: Tests & CI
tour.description: A large pytest suite locks in world physics, agent behavior, evolution, reward shaping, and config handling; GitHub Actions runs lint, type-check, and the full test suite on every change.
